headline: Orchestrate Advanced Code Reviews with the Codex Plugin
lede: This plugin bridges Claude Code and the Codex ecosystem to facilitate complex task delegation. It enables deep-dive adversarial reviews and structured architectural validation within your terminal workflow.

headline: Shrink Agent Token Usage by Sixty Percent with Caveman
lede: Caveman enforces a high-density communication protocol that strips away conversational filler from AI assistants. By optimizing output brevity, it directly lowers API overhead while maintaining technical accuracy.

headline: Control Web Interfaces Using Natural Language Commands
lede: Page Agent enables direct DOM manipulation via JavaScript without requiring headless browser dependencies. It empowers developers to build seamless, intent-based automation directly into existing web applications.

headline: Automate Penetration Testing with the Strix Orchestration Engine
lede: Strix deploys multi-agent teams to perform autonomous reconnaissance and generate functional exploit proofs. By shifting security validation into the CI/CD pipeline, it identifies critical vulnerabilities before production deployment.

headline: Connect AI Agents Directly to Browser Internals
lede: This MCP server bridges LLMs with the Chrome DevTools protocol to provide real-time browser visibility. It allows agents to autonomously analyze performance traces, console logs, and network traffic.

headline: Summarize Meetings Locally with the Meetily Assistant
lede: Meetily ensures data sovereignty by processing audio transcriptions and summaries entirely on local hardware. Built with Rust and Tauri, this tool provides a secure, private alternative for handling sensitive enterprise discussions.

headline: Track Evolution of Proprietary System Instructions
lede: This repository provides a crowdsourced, structured index of the system prompts governing major AI models. It offers critical insights into the behavioral constraints and alignment techniques defining modern LLM interactions.

headline: Standardize AI Systems Engineering with Harvard Curriculum
lede: This integrated curriculum moves beyond model training to address the infrastructure required for robust intelligent systems. It provides a technical framework for managing data, hardware constraints, and fleet orchestration at scale.

headline: Manage Retro Gaming Libraries with the RomM Interface
lede: RomM transforms fragmented ROM collections into an organized, metadata-rich digital library. Its modular design supports hundreds of systems, providing a centralized hub for both archival and immediate play.

headline: Organize Multi-Agent Workflows in the Terminal with Herdr
lede: Herdr provides a terminal-based multiplexer designed specifically for managing concurrent AI agent sessions. Its client-server architecture ensures process persistence and efficient task monitoring within a single command-line interface.
